visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) Intro split screen phase: The left side shows a macro view of blueberries and strawberries being poured into a large glass bowl filled with ice and a pinkish liquid. The right side shows a young woman with long dark hair and a red sweater eating candies, her eyes wide with surprise. (2) Preparation phase: The woman is seated in an indoor room with a colorful 'HAPPINESS IS OPENING' poster. She shows a large glass bowl of ice, then adds a container of blueberries followed by a bowl of strawberries. The lighting is bright and studio-like. (3) Mixing phase: She pours a cup of dark liquid and pearls from a yellow CoCo cup into the bowl, then opens a green can of Sprite and pours it in, creating a bubbly mixture. (4) Consumption phase: The woman begins eating from the bowl using a large metal ladle. She first eats slices of wax apple, then scoops up large portions of berries and pearls, showing exaggerated facial expressions of enjoyment. (5) Endorsement phase: She presents a yellow 'NEAR' product box and small green packets. She opens one, eats the contents, and points to her jawline while smiling at the camera.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Subtitle]: 韓國最新草莓吃法. [Slogan]: 草莓加「珍珠雪碧」?. (2) [Slogan]: 實驗歐膩. (3) [Subtitle]: 今天也準備大玻璃碗. (4) [Subtitle]: 好多人問我「韓國人為何冬天也愛吃冰的? 」. (5) [Subtitle]: 其實我也不知道為什麼就DNA那樣(? ). (6) [Subtitle]: 加草莓. (7) [Subtitle]: 這個用韓語叫做Hwachae(花菜). (8) [Subtitle]: 其實原本是要加草莓牛奶跟雪碧. (9) [Subtitle]: 但台灣就不一樣啊!. (10) [Subtitle]: 剛剛影片那樣要加珍珠草莓手搖飲. (11) [Subtitle]: 一定要加「珍珠」. (12) [Subtitle]: 爆多. (13) [Subtitle]: 我買的是CoCo的草莓飲料. (14) [Subtitle]: 韓國花菜一定要加雪碧喔~. (15) [Variety]: (還有水果? ). (16) [Subtitle]: 蓮霧好好吃喔. (17) [Subtitle]: 珍珠 + 草莓 + 藍莓. (18) [Subtitle]: 其實沒期待了 但好好吃喔. (19) [Subtitle]: 藍莓+珍珠必須加!!! 口感超有趣. (20) [Variety]: 爽爽. [Variety]: 清清爽爽. (21) [Subtitle]: 好想加酒喝(????? ). (22) [Subtitle]: 過冬天前可以先多吃草莓!. (23) [Subtitle]: 吃宵夜後記得吃V糖. (24) [Subtitle]: 是Sana也愛吃這個. (25) [Subtitle]: 超好吃+可以管理V-Line. (26) [Subtitle]: 吃完東西再吃這個的話都不怕宵夜. 早上滿月臉!.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main creator (female), 1 secondary person visible in the intro split screen.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long, wavy black hair, wearing a vibrant red or pinkish knitted sweater.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: An indoor room setup as a content creation studio, featuring a colorful poster with the text 'HAPPINESS IS OPENING' and 'HELLO' in the background.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) Intro phase: An upbeat, high-tempo electronic dance track begins immediately. A female voice speaks with a bright, high-arousal tone. The sharp sound of ice cubes hitting glass is audible underneath the narration. (2) Pouring phase: A distinct splashing sound of liquid is heard twice as ingredients are added. This is accompanied by a 'Ding' sound effect for emphasis. The music continues its steady, driving beat. (3) Fizzing phase: A loud, metallic click occurs as a soda can is opened, followed by a crisp 'pssh' sound and a few seconds of active fizzing as the carbonated liquid hits the ice. (4) Chewing phase: The speaker's voice becomes punctuated by prominent, crunchy chewing and swallowing sounds (ASMR-style). Rhythmic 'Pop' sound effects are used to mirror the texture of the pearls. (5) Wrapping phase: The crinkling sound of a plastic packet being opened is clearly audible. The music remains at a consistent volume until a final high-pitched vocal exclamation and a hard cut to silence.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To demonstrate and review a viral Korean fruit dessert (Hwachae) modified with local Taiwanese ingredients (bubble tea pearls), followed by a promotional segment for a dietary supplement.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The creator specifically pauses the food review to show a product box (NEAR), opens a packet, eats it, and explains its benefits for 'V-Line' management after eating late-night snacks.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video successfully blends trend-chasing food content with a commercial endorsement. The high production energy and fast-paced editing are typical for social media engagement.
